name: Phoenix
metaTitle: Phoenix Accident Guide — Local Hospitals, Courts & Rights
metaDescription: Injured in Phoenix? Find local emergency hospitals, courts, and understand your rights under Arizona personal injury law.
description: Phoenix is the largest city in Arizona and one of the fastest-growing major cities in the United States. Its expansive grid of arterial roads and freeway system — including the I-10, US-60, Loop 101, Loop 202, and SR-51 — carries enormous daily traffic. Phoenix has a high rate of pedestrian fatalities, consistently ranking among the most dangerous cities in the nation for pedestrians due to wide, high-speed roads and significant heat-related visibility issues. The city's booming construction sector also means a steady volume of workplace and construction site accidents. Government claims against the City of Phoenix or Maricopa County must be filed within 180 days under Arizona law (A.R.S. § 12-821.01). Phoenix also has a large rideshare and delivery vehicle presence contributing to urban accident volume.

hospitals.name: Dignity Health St. Joseph's Hospital and Medical Center

courts.name: Maricopa County Superior Court — Southeast Facility

commonAccidentTypes: Pedestrian accidents on high-speed arterial roads (Camelback, Van Buren, Indian School)
Freeway accidents on I-10 and Loop 101 interchanges
Rear-end accidents at traffic signals on major grid roads
Construction site accidents in the active development corridor
DUI-related accidents — Arizona has among the nation's strictest DUI laws
Rideshare and delivery vehicle accidents in the urban core
notableCorridors: I-10 (Maricopa Freeway) — primary east-west corridor, high truck and commuter volume
Loop 101 (Pima Freeway / Price Freeway) — suburban beltway with high accident frequency
US-60 (Superstition Freeway) — East Valley commuter corridor
SR-51 (Piestewa Freeway) — north-south urban freeway through central Phoenix
Van Buren Street — pedestrian high-risk corridor through downtown Phoenix
Camelback Road — major arterial with mixed pedestrian and high-speed vehicle traffic
localNotes: Phoenix has a 180-day deadline for government entity claims under Arizona law. Phoenix PD traffic accident reports are available through the Records Management Bureau. The Valley Metro light rail system is a common government defendant in transit injury cases. Arizona's extreme heat creates specific hazard conditions — melting road markings, heat shimmer affecting visibility, and increased tire blowout rates — that may be relevant to accident liability.
